channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ondo Pastors Allege Extortion and Threats By Police Over Planned Crusade
item.description: Some pastors for Ondo State, including Olarewaju Fadahunsi, Gbadura Marvelous, Arijesulola Kayode, Tijani, and Kolade, don claim say dem experience serious wahala from police under Commissioner Adebow...

item.title: ODM Leaders Reject Registration of Linda Mwananchi as a New Political Party
item.description: Leaders allied to the Linda Mwananchi faction within the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), including Nairobi senator Edwin Sifuna and Embakasi East MP Babu Owino, have officially opposed attempts to r...

item.title: Indian Woman Recounts Experience During Dubai Crisis and Evacuation
item.description: An Indian national, Saumya Khandelwal, who travelled to Dubai for work, recently returned to India in the first batch of evacuation flights after air travel resumed amid Middle East tensions. She desc...

item.title: Tanzania authorities seize large drug consignments and arrest traffickers nationwide
item.description: The Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) in Tanzania has carried out a major crackdown on illegal drugs across the country. Working with officers at the Mutukula Customs Post, the DCEA interc...

item.title: US Military Kills Iranian Official Allegedly Behind 2024 Assassination Plot Against Trump
item.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that a US military operation killed an Iranian official accused of orchestrating an assassination attempt on Donald Trump in 2024. The operation targeted th...

item.title: Ontario Education Minister Proposes Standardized Policies Amid Concerns Over Underfunding and Class Sizes
item.description: Ontario's Education Minister, Paul Calandra, has signalled plans to standardize curriculum across the province and reintroduce paper report cards, aiming to simplify communication with parents and add...

item.title: LTO issues show cause order for passengers on government pick-up truck bed
item.description: The Land Transportation Office (LTO) has issued a show cause order (SCO) against the driver and registered owner of a government-owned pick-up truck after a photo went viral showing three passengers s...

item.title: Nigerian Authorities Report No Citizens in Iran Have Requested Evacuation Amid Middle-East Conflict
item.description: The Nigerian government dey monitor the ongoing tension for Middle East, especially wey concern Iran, USA and Israel. According to the Nigerians in Diaspora Commission (NiDCOM), no Nigerian wey dey Ir...

item.title: President Tinubu Suspends FAAN Cashless Payment Policy at Nigerian Airports Over Gridlock Complaints
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has directed the Minister of Aviation, Festus Keyamo SAN, to immediately suspend the cashless payment policy at the gates and payment points of Nigerian airports. This deci...

item.title: US Submarine Sinks Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka, Dozens Killed
item.description: A US submarine has torpedoed and sunk the Iranian warship IRIS Dena off the coast of Sri Lanka, resulting in at least 80 fatalities. The incident represents a significant escalation in tensions betwee...

item.title: Scottish Labour MP's Husband Arrested Over Alleged China Espionage; MP Denies Involvement
item.description: Scottish Labour MP Joani Reid has stated that she has never observed any behaviour suggesting her husband, David Taylor, has committed a crime, following his arrest on suspicion of spying for China al...

item.title: Zimbabwe Rejects $367m US Health Pact Over Sovereignty Concerns, Faces Aid Cuts
item.description: Zimbabwe President has decided to reject a $367 million health agreement with the United States, saying the deal would compromise Zimbabwe’s control over important data and lithium resources. This mov...

item.title: US-Israel and Iran Conflict Escalates in Syria
item.description: As of March 5, 2026, tensions in the Middle East have escalated with ongoing conflicts involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. Recent airstrikes have targeted areas in Syria, including near Qam...

item.title: Melbourne Grand Prix Launch Party Returns to Glamour and Elegance
item.description: The 10th anniversary of the Melbourne Grand Prix launch event, Glamour on the Grid, marked a return to elegance and exclusivity after several years dominated by social media influencers, often dubbed ...

item.title: Revised sea level measurements show more people at risk from coastal flooding
item.description: A recent study published in the journal Nature reveals that previous estimates of coastal water heights have been significantly underestimated, placing millions more people at risk from rising sea lev...

item.title: Nigerian Youth Encouraged to Develop Skills for Global Opportunities
item.description: For Port Harcourt, the Fasthire CareerFest 2026 don hold ground wey gather over 900 participants including Corps members, career coaches, and young entrepreneurs. Richard Okiasi, the convener, talk sa...

item.title: Security Forces Neutralise 23 Terrorists Linked to Indian Proxy Group in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Operations
item.description: According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Pakistan's security forces conducted successful intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on November 16 and 17, targeting ter...

item.title: IAEA Approves Safeguards for Chashma Unit-5, Pakistan to Strengthen Low-Carbon Energy
item.description: ISLAMABAD – The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) board of governors has formally approved safeguards for Chashma Unit-5, Pakistan’s upcoming 1,200 MW pressurised water reactor, according to t...

item.title: Ethiopia to build Nakuwa Bridge, boosting peace and trade at Kenya border
item.description: Joy is spreading among communities in Turkana North and Kibish near the Kenya-Ethiopia border following Ethiopia's commitment to build the Nakuwa Bridge. For many years, families in the Ateker border ...

item.title: US Senate Votes Against War Powers Resolution to Stop Military Action in Iran
item.description: On 4th March 2026, the U.S. Senate witnessed a significant vote on a war powers resolution aimed at halting military operations in Iran. The resolution, which required congressional approval for any f...
